text:
the market
CAG: severe acute colitis
ASCA: anti-Saccharomyces cerevisiae antibodies
AZA: azathioprine
CRP: C reactive protein
CSP: primary sclerosing cholangitis
EBV: Epstein Barr virus
NEE: exclusive enteral nutrition
EO-IBD: early onset inflammatory bowel disease
IBD: inflammatory bowel disease
IMC: body mass index
IFX: infliximab
IRM: magnetic resonance imaging
LM: modified release
MC: Crohn disease
MICI: chronic inflammatory bowel disease
MTX: methotrexate
NFS: complete blood count
p-ANCA: antibodies directed against the perinuclear region of the cytoplasm of neutrophil polymorphonuclear cells
PCDAI: pediatric Crohn disease activity index
PO: orally
PUCAI: pediatric ulcerative colitis activity index
RCH: ulcerative colitis
SES-CD: simple endoscopic score for Crohn disease
TNF: tumor necrosis factor
TPMT: thiopurine methyltransferase
VEO-IBD: very early onset inflammatory bowel disease
Introduction
IBD can be diagnosed throughout life. 
However, there are specific features unique to the pediatric
form. For example, Crohn disease is more often 
diagnosed at the inflammatory stage,
